user_query:
I’m preparing a column on how Tom Hanks’ films use shot duration and palette to shape tone. Please turn the attached notes and scene-level metrics into a small, reproducible analysis and communication package with the following outputs:

1) Compute per-film style metrics from input/scene_metrics.csv and save a summary table to outputs/film_style_summary.csv with this exact column order and names:
- film
- year
- n_scenes
- mean_shot_length_sec
- std_shot_length_sec
- interior_mean_shot_length_sec
- exterior_mean_shot_length_sec
- mean_color_temp_k
- mean_saturation
- mean_brightness
- mean_dialog_sentiment

Rules for the summary:
- Group by film and year.
- Use all rows.
- Round mean_shot_length_sec, std_shot_length_sec, interior_mean_shot_length_sec, and exterior_mean_shot_length_sec to 2 decimals; round mean_color_temp_k to the nearest integer; round mean_saturation, mean_brightness, and mean_dialog_sentiment to 2 decimals.

2) Rewrite input/critic_notes.txt into a neutral, professional 120–150 word abstract and place it as the Abstract section at the top of outputs/report.md. The abstract must improve clarity and concision, avoid hype or slang, and must not reuse any string of more than 15 consecutive words from the notes.

3) Create outputs/report.md with the following sections in order: Abstract, Methods, Results, Interpretation.
- Methods (2–4 sentences): briefly describe how you computed the metrics from the CSV and the rounding applied.
- Results (bullet list): include at least four bullets. Two bullets must appear exactly once each and must reflect the computed summary:
  • Shortest mean shot length: <film> (<value>s)
  • Highest mean color temperature: <film> (<value>K)
  Use the 2-decimal rounded value for shot length and the integer-rounded value for color temperature taken from outputs/film_style_summary.csv. The remaining bullets should cite at least two other quantitative comparisons drawn from the summary (e.g., interior vs. exterior mean shot length for a film).
- Interpretation (120–180 words): connect the quantitative patterns to artistic choices in these films in a measured, evidence-based tone.

4) Draft a concise email in outputs/email_draft.txt using input/email_context.yaml. The email should be 120–180 words, addressed to the specified recipient, summarize one or two headline findings (e.g., the bullets above), and politely request a quick methodological sanity check by the provided deadline. Match the tone guidance and mention that the attached report (outputs/report.md) contains the details.

Use only the provided input files and save all outputs under the specified paths. Ensure all numeric claims in the Results section align with outputs/film_style_summary.csv. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
